visual_features.overall_style: Modern, minimalist documentation UI with a light theme, neutral typography, and subtle brand color accents. It feels SaaS/DevTools-oriented with a focus on clarity and readability over visual decoration.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven by typography, color, and spacing. The top global header and search bar form the first level. Within the main column, the purple section label ('Guides') and large bold H1 ('Evaluation') dominate, followed by a lighter-weight subtitle and body copy. Links and key actions use stronger color (purple/blue) to stand out from mostly neutral text. In the left sidebar, section headings are bold with more spacing; the active nav item is highlighted with a colored pill. On the right, the 'On this page' label is subtly emphasized with an icon and stronger contrast, while nested anchor links use indentation and lighter color to create sub-hierarchy.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Generous whitespace around all three columns and between major sections. The central content column has comfortable margins from both sidebars, and paragraphs have clear line spacing and vertical separation. Navigation items have enough padding to be easily scannable without feeling cramped. Overall, whitespace is used to reduce cognitive load and support a relaxed reading experience.
unique_elements: Triple-column documentation layout: left primary navigation, center article, right in-page table of contents.
Pill-shaped active state in the left sidebar with a soft lavender gradient, giving a distinct but subtle selection cue.
Pill-shaped, search-as-command-palette bar centered in the header including a keyboard shortcut hint ('Ctrl K').
Dark, high-contrast primary CTA button in the header ('HomePage') using a deep purple pill that contrasts with the otherwise light header.
Icon-based utility nav items in the left sidebar (Github/Discord/Chat with Founders) styled like compact list items with left-aligned circular icons.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implied 12-column grid with three main vertical regions: fixed-width left sidebar, fluid central content column, and narrower right sidebar.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Documentation layout with a fixed global header, left navigation sidebar, central article content, and right in-page navigation (TOC).
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top global header spanning full width. Below, a three-column content area: left sidebar for documentation navigation, center for the current guide, right sidebar for anchor links within the current document. Within the center column, content flows top-to-bottom: context label, H1, subtitle, introduction text, then media content (screenshot).
layout_characteristics.alignment: Primarily left-aligned text and components. Vertical alignment is consistently top-aligned for columns. The search bar is horizontally centered in the header while nav items and logo align left and right, producing a balanced layout.
responsive_hints: Sidebars appear fixed-width, suggesting that on smaller viewports they likely collapse into drawers or shift below the main content.
Central content column appears constrained to a readable max-width, hinting that it will remain center-aligned with margins on larger screens.
The pill search bar in the header likely reflows or shrinks to fit smaller widths, potentially converting into an icon-triggered command palette.
Right-hand 'On this page' TOC is non-essential and likely hides or moves below content on mobile for vertical-only scrolling.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Content follows a linear top-to-bottom flow within each column. In the main (center) column, the order is: page category label ('Guides') in small colored text, large page title (H1), short subtitle, then a multi-line introductory paragraph. Below the text, a full-width media block (screenshot) spans the width of the content column. The left sidebar arranges items in a vertical stack: top-level tabs (Documentation/API Reference/Blog), then vertical nav items grouped by labeled sections ('Welcome', 'Guides', etc.) with nested links indented under each. The right sidebar arranges the in-page TOC in a vertical list, using indentation to denote heading levels and subtle typography differences to separate main sections from sub-items.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Approximate width distribution is about 20–25% for the left sidebar, 50–55% for the central content, and 20–25% for the right sidebar on this viewport. The global header occupies the full width but modest vertical height with comfortable padding. Within the central column, top spacing above the H1 and between elements (label, title, subtitle, paragraph, screenshot) is generous, prioritizing readability over packing more elements per viewport.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately spacious. The main article text is low-density with generous line-height and margins, typical of developer documentation optimized for reading. Navigation lists (left and right) are moderately dense but padded enough to be scannable without looking busy. No overlapping or tightly packed elements; sections breathe with clear vertical separation.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye typically moves in a Z-pattern from the top-left logo to the centered search bar, then to the top-right CTA button. From there, attention drops diagonally to the left sidebar to locate the section, then into the central H1 title and introductory text. The highlighted active navigation pill and purple label draw the eye to the current context. After reading the main intro content, secondary focus may shift to the right-hand 'On this page' list for scanning subtopics, creating a secondary left-to-right flow across the three columns.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary button (e.g., 'HomePage') appears as a pill-shaped rectangle with fully rounded corners (~999px radius). It uses a deep, almost black-purple background (#1E0B3A) with white text, medium-to-bold weight. Padding is generous (approx. 10–12px vertical, 20–24px horizontal). Text is horizontally centered with a small arrow/icon on the right separated by 4–8px. The button is flat or has a very subtle shadow and no visible border, relying on color contrast and shape for prominence. Secondary actions (like sidebar pills) use lighter backgrounds with low-contrast text and no heavy shadows.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typically implemented as either a .

ui_patterns.visual_description: The primary media/content area (screenshot) is shown within a rectangular container with sharp or slightly rounded corners (~4–6px). It appears flush with the white background, with possibly a faint border or drop-shadow from the embedded image rather than the frame itself. Internal padding is minimal for the screenshot; it likely touches or nearly touches the card edges. Overall style is flat, focusing on the content rather than decorative framing.

. For other parts of the site (not shown), a consistent pattern would be a bordered or lightly shadowed box with padding and vertically stacked elements inside.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The visible form element is the search bar. It is a large pill-shaped input with full rounding (~999px radius), white background, light gray border (#E5E7EB), and subtle inner spacing. Left side has a search icon inside the same field, right side has a small muted text badge ('Ctrl K'). Placeholder text ('Search...') is medium gray (#9CA3AF). Overall, it appears flat and clean, with focus likely indicated by a darker border or glow.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Implemented as a form wrapper with an plus inline icons and keyboard-hint text. Likely structure:
Ctrl K
. Elements are arranged with flexbox for horizontal alignment.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Global navigation is a horizontal bar at the top with logo on the left, three text links ('Documentation', 'API Reference', 'Blog') centered or left-middle, and utility items (Discord link, primary CTA, theme toggle) on the right. Active top-level nav item uses darker text and possibly an underline or bottom border. Left sidebar navigation is vertical with section titles in bold, small uppercase or all-caps styling, and grouped links below. Active link is wrapped in a rounded rectangle with soft lavender background and slightly stronger text color. Right sidebar navigation ('On this page') is a vertical list of anchors with small fonts, using bold and purple for the active item and lighter gray for inactive items. Indentation conveys heading levels. All navigation appears flat with subtle hover color changes instead of heavy decoration.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Sidebar lists (both left navigation groups and the right-hand TOC) are vertically stacked items with consistent spacing (~6–10px between items). They are low-density for readability. There are no visible divider lines; separation is achieved with spacing and occasional background pills. Icons (e.g., for Github, Discord) appear in a circular or rounded square background to the left of list text. Text hierarchy uses weight and color: labels in bold/darker, supporting text in regular/lighter grey.

heading.visual_style: Clean, neutral sans-serif with a relatively high x-height and slightly heavier weight for headings, likely a modern grotesque. It appears straightforward and tech-oriented rather than decorative.

body.visual_style: Readable, humanist-style sans-serif with clear letterforms and moderate stroke contrast, optimized for long-form reading in documentation.

spacing_system.component_padding: Common paddings appear to be 8–12px vertical and 16–24px horizontal for interactive elements like buttons and nav pills; 16–24px internal padding for larger blocks such as cards or sidebar containers.
spacing_system.section_margin: Major vertical section spacing (e.g., between header and first content block, or between paragraphs and media) appears around 24–40px, with larger 48–64px gaps between major conceptual sections of documentation pages.

interaction_hints: Interactive elements (buttons, nav links, TOC items) likely use hover states that slightly darken background colors or increase text contrast while preserving the flat aesthetic.
Active navigation items use both color and background shape changes (lavender pill for sidebar, purple text for TOC) to signal current context.
Search bar likely shows a stronger border or shadow on focus and might trigger a modal/command palette when activated or when pressing Ctrl+K.
Smooth scrolling to anchors when clicking right-hand TOC links is a natural fit, supporting orientation in long documentation pages.
Subtle 150–200ms ease-in-out transitions on color and background changes would match the calm, modern feel without feeling flashy.
